question: Tell me about Right to Education
answer:
The Right to Education is guaranteed under Article 21A of the Constitution, which states that "The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine" (Article 21A). This means that every child between the ages of 6 and 14 has the right to receive free and compulsory education, and the State is responsible for providing it.

question: What was the special status of Jammu and Kashmir under Article 370
answer:
Under Article 370 of the Constitution of India, the special status of Jammu and Kashmir meant that the State had a certain degree of autonomy. According to Article 370(1)(b), the power of Parliament to make laws for the State of Jammu and Kashmir was limited to matters in the Union List and the Concurrent List (Part XXI, Article 370, Clause 1(b)(i)). Additionally, the provisions of Article 238 did not apply to the State of Jammu and Kashmir (Part XXI, Article 370, Clause 1(a)).

question: Tell me about Emergency Provisions
answer: Emergency Provisions are outlined in Part XVIII of the Constitution of India. According to Article 359, during emergencies, the enforcement of the rights conferred by Part III can be suspended. The President can proclaim an emergency under certain circumstances, and this proclamation can extend to the whole or any part of the territory of India (Article 211). question: Tell me about Women Safety
answer: Women safety is ensured through the provisions of the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013. According to Section 19, every employer is required to provide a safe working environment at the workplace, which includes safety from persons coming into contact at the workplace.
